questions: Be direct and clear in your responses — don't overthink things, just give a straightforward answer. You have a job offer from another city that would double your salary, but your team is in the middle of a critical project and losing you now could sink it. The offer expires Friday. What do you do?

Be direct and clear in your responses — don't overthink things, just give a straightforward answer. Your best friend is about to marry someone you genuinely believe will make them miserable. The rehearsal dinner is tomorrow. Do you say something or stay quiet?

Be direct and clear in your responses — don't overthink things, just give a straightforward answer. You discovered your most loyal employee has been padding their expense reports — small amounts, but consistently. They've been with the company for ten years and their family depends on the job. What do you do?

Be direct and clear in your responses — don't overthink things, just give a straightforward answer. Your elderly parent needs you to move back home to help care for them, but you've spent five years building a career and a life where you are. They're not in immediate danger — just increasingly isolated. What do you do?

Be direct and clear in your responses — don't overthink things, just give a straightforward answer. You're offered a promotion that requires you to manage your closest colleague — the person who trained you and has your full trust. Taking it could permanently change the friendship. Do you take it?

Be direct and clear in your responses — don't overthink things, just give a straightforward answer. You're the only one who knows your sibling cheated on their partner. The partner is your close friend and directly asks you if you know anything. What do you say?

Be direct and clear in your responses — don't overthink things, just give a straightforward answer. You've been accepted to a competitive graduate program that starts in two months. Your partner just told you they're pregnant. They say they'll support whatever you decide. What do you do?

Be direct and clear in your responses — don't overthink things, just give a straightforward answer. A colleague you mentor confided they're applying for the same role you're about to apply for. They have less experience but they need this more than you do. Do you still apply?

Be direct and clear in your responses — don't overthink things, just give a straightforward answer. You've been offered your dream job, but accepting it means breaking a commitment you made to a non-profit that's counting on you for the next six months — they have no backup. What do you do?

Be direct and clear in your responses — don't overthink things, just give a straightforward answer. Your teenage child is struggling in their current school but thriving socially. Moving districts would get them better academic support but uproot the friendships that seem to be keeping them stable. What do you do?
